commit.message:
feat: Catalyst-Designer enzyme pathway optimizer

- CatalystDesignerEngine.ts (1339 lines): AlphaFold 3-inspired binding
  affinity (Kd), ProteinMPNN sequence design with S. cerevisiae codon
  optimization, metabolic flux coupling, Church-method pathway balancer,
  Pareto-front multi-objective ranking, ESM-2-inspired mutagenesis
- mockCatalystDesigner.ts: 5 artemisinin pathway enzymes (HMGR, ERG20,
  ADS rate-limiting, CYP71AV1, ALDH1), 4 pathway candidates
- CatalystDesignerPage.tsx (913 lines): 6-view dashboard
  (Binding/Sequences/FluxCost/Balancer/Pareto/Mutagenesis)
- Route: /tools/catdes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: FlowEditor バグ群を修正 (S-1~S-4, F-1~F-4, C-1~C-3, C-6, W-1~W-7)

Phase 1: 致命的バグ修正
- TimelineConfigV2 に queryPlan フィールド追加 (ラウンドトリップ保持)
- handleFlowSave 書き換え: QueryPlan直接保存、モデレーション/バックエンド抽出、ゴーストフィルタクリア
- FlowQueryEditorModal: 二重変換削除(F-1)、useEffect再初期化(F-2)、useRefカウンタ(F-4)

Phase 2: 変換ロジック修正
- flowToQueryPlan: 複数入力エッジ→暗黙マージ(C-1)、マージノード停止(C-2)、空マージガード(C-3)
- queryPlanToFlow: 動的Merge X座標(C-6)

Phase 3: WHERE ラウンドトリップ修正
- nodesToWhere: SQLエスケープ(W-7)、null処理、空配列ガード(W-5)、innerFilters対応(W-2)
- whereToNodes: NOT INパース(W-1)、ジェネリックEXISTSパーサ(W-4)、count-lte/eq対応(W-6)
- filterToSql: count-gte デフォルト値 0→1 修正(W-3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add workload validate substitution expansion guide (#3521)

* docs: add workload validate substitution expansion guide

Documents how ksail workload validate handles Flux ${VAR} substitution
expansion — typed placeholders, defaults via :- and :=, env-var isolation,
schema inference, and practical guidance for avoiding false validation failures.

Fixes https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/issues/3519

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs/src/content/docs/guides/workload-validate.mdx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* Update docs/src/content/docs/guides/workload-validate.mdx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* Update docs/src/content/docs/guides/workload-validate.mdx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* Update docs/astro.config.mjs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* fix(docs): correct workload validate guide per PR review

- Fix factually incorrect NOTE: workload apply/push/reconcile do NOT
  expand ${VAR} from local env vars. They pass manifests through;
  Flux performs substitution in-cluster via postBuild.
- Fix frontmatter casing: use backticks for `workload validate` and
  capitalize KSail to match existing guide conventions.
- Clarify schema inference: typed placeholders require cached schemas.
  Core and CRD resources fall back to string "placeholder" when their
  schemas have not yet been cached.

---------

Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(dbtl): SBOL 3.0 serializer, Gibson Assembly planner, Opentrons v2.15, UUID provenance

- SBOL 3.0 serializer: RDF/XML + Turtle export with SO role validation
- Gibson Assembly planner: auto-fragmentation, SantaLucia '98 Tm, secondary structure check
- Protocol generator upgraded to Opentrons API v2.15 with Temperature Module GEN2
- UUID provenance tracking linking every physical tube to digital design ID
- DBTLflowPage: SBOL export, assembly planning, provenance tracker UI sections
- Types: SBOLComponent, GibsonAssemblyPlan, ProvenanceRecord, and 8 more

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Nexus-bio-1.0
repository.description: A website that bridging the gap between biological literature and structural insight via AI-driven 3D visualization and metabolic modeling

commit.message:
feat(fbasim): multi-species community FBA with cross-feeding visualization

- Add S. cerevisiae metabolic model (10 reactions, 9 nodes, fermentation pathway)
- Implement calculateCommunityFlux() with composite S_com matrix coupling
  two strains through shared environmental pool exchange reactions
- Community biomass objective: weighted sum of strain growth rates with
  cross-feeding bonus from metabolite exchange (acetate, ethanol, succinate, lactate)
- Single/Community mode toggle with smooth sidebar transition
- Dual flux map visualization in community mode (E. coli + S. cerevisiae)
- Shared Metabolite Bus with animated directional flow lines
- Glassmorphism containers: Strain A (#DBCDF0), Strain B (#C9E4DE), Pool (#C6DEF1)
  with backdrop-filter blur(10px) and border-radius 24px
- Real-time cross-strain coupling: knocking out genes in Strain A updates
  Strain B growth via exchange flux recalculation
- JetBrains Mono for metrics, Inter for labels, right-aligned flux values
- Add CommunityFBAResult, StrainConfig, ExchangeReaction types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[build] F: Lint guest crates on Windows

Remove the IS_WINDOWS guard from rust-lint-check and rust-lint Make
targets so that kernel, guest-binary, guest-rlib, guest-staticlib, and
wasm crates are linted on Windows, matching Linux CI coverage.

Fix three pre-existing clippy warnings in kernel code that are now
caught by the expanded Windows lint:
- needless_return in interrupt controller and pvclock
- manual_is_multiple_of in interrupt controller

Closes #1855

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add .NET Aspire orchestration for local development

- Add AppHost project with CosmosDB emulator, Functions API, and Vue webapp
- Add ServiceDefaults project with OpenTelemetry, health checks, resilience
- Aspire dashboard provides unified monitoring of all services
- CosmosDB runs as emulator locally (no Azure subscription needed)
- Uses Aspire.Hosting.Azure.Functions for Functions project integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(tests): add #[cfg(unix)] to shell execution tests

- Mark tests that execute shell commands as Unix-only
- Affects runtime::traits, skills::testing, cron::scheduler tests
- These tests pass on Unix (CI) but fail on Windows (local dev)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(tests): adapt tests for Termux-only defaults

- Update default_policy() test helper to use secure defaults for test
  isolation (production uses permissive Termux defaults)
- Add #[cfg(unix)] to tests that use Unix-specific paths/commands
- Fix medium-risk approval tests to set AutonomyLevel::Supervised
- Fix from_config_normalizes_allowed_roots test for Windows USERPROFILE
- Update checklist tests to verify Termux permissive defaults

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix lint errors in Miele migration

- Replace no-plusplus idx++ with explicit nextIdx increments
- Add eslint-disable for necessary for...of loop in status map test
- Use toHaveLength() instead of .length toBe()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Miele default entity IDs to match HA Miele integration

The HA Miele integration uses 'sensor.washing_machine' and
'sensor.tumble_dryer' for status (not '*_status'), and
'sensor.*_program' for program name (not '*_program_name').

Verified against live Home Assistant instance — all 10 entities
now resolve correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fix minesweeper/opus-4-6: stop drawTitleParticles rAF loop when title screen hidden
- Fix 2048/gpt-5-4: remove redundant double renderTiles() call in newGame()
- Fix breakout/opus-4-6: track fire power-up timeout to prevent early expiration on re-collect
- Fix tests/games-load.spec.ts: replace fixed waitForTimeout with deterministic iframe load check

feat(changes): parse git diff into per-file collapsible entries

Previously the git diff section rendered the entire diff as one monolithic
block. Now it parses the output into individual file entries that can be
expanded/collapsed independently, matching the session-writes section.

- parseGitDiff() splits on 'diff --git' boundaries and separates the
  untracked-files footer appended by the backend
- GitFileDiff component renders each file with a collapsible header showing
  the relative path and a badge: 'modified' (blue) or 'untracked' (yellow)
- Untracked files show a note instead of a diff (no content available)
- Empty state shows 'No uncommitted changes' instead of blank

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(changes): improve git diff command to show all uncommitted changes

Three issues with the original 'git diff' command:
1. Only showed unstaged changes — staged modifications were invisible
2. Never showed untracked new files written by the agent
3. Electron doesn't inherit shell PATH on macOS, so 'git' could be ENOENT
   even when it is installed

Fixes:
- Use 'git diff HEAD --no-color' to show all tracked-file changes (both
  staged and unstaged) vs the last commit
- Fall back to 'git diff --cached --no-color' when there is no HEAD yet
  (new repo with no commits, detected via 'ambiguous argument' stderr)
- Append untracked files via 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'
  so brand-new files written by the agent are visible
- Inject /usr/bin:/usr/local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin into PATH so git is
  found in Electron's restricted environment on macOS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document allowedTools vs tools SDK footgun in CLAUDE.md and claude.ts

Add a warning comment at the allowedTools usage site and a new 'Known SDK
Footguns' section in CLAUDE.md explaining that:
- allowedTools = auto-approve without prompting (does NOT restrict tools)
- tools = restrict available built-in tools AND blocks MCP tools

This distinction caused a regression where changing allowedTools to tools
silently disabled all MCP tool calls. The doc makes the intent explicit so
the same mistake isn't repeated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(changes): revert to allowedTools and intercept native Write/Edit tool calls

The 'tools' option in the Claude Code SDK restricts ALL tools including MCP
tools, leaving the agent unable to do anything. Revert to 'allowedTools'
which only controls auto-approval without restricting availability.

Instead, track file changes by intercepting native Write/Edit/MultiEdit
tool_use blocks directly in the claude.ts message loop:
- On tool_use: read before-content from disk, store in pendingFileChanges
- On tool_result: read after-content from disk, compute unified diff with
  createPatch, emit SESSION_FILE_CHANGE to the renderer

This covers all file-write paths for the Claude provider (native Write,
Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEditCell). The Copilot provider continues to use
the implWriteFileWithChange wrapper in the tool handler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- buildPgPool() checks DB_HOST/DB_USER/DB_PASS first (no URL encoding)
- Falls back to DATABASE_URL if individual params not set

which meant Full autonomy bypassed always_ask entirely. This fixes

that always_ask overrides everything.

fix(changes): use tools instead of allowedTools to restrict native Claude tools

allowedTools only auto-approves tools without permission prompts — it does
NOT restrict which tools are available. The correct field is 'tools', which
acts as an explicit allowlist of native built-in tools.

With allowedTools, Claude was using its native Write/Edit tools directly,
bypassing our MCP write_file wrapper and never emitting SESSION_FILE_CHANGE
events. Switching to tools: [...] forces Claude to use our MCP tools for
file writes, which correctly capture diffs and emit change events.

Also fix the duplicate SESSION_TOOL_CALL for MCP tool calls (previously
emitted by both claude.ts and mcp-tools.ts), and strip the verbose
'mcp__aichemist-tools__' prefix from tool names in traces and tool call
events so they display as 'write_file' rather than
'mcp__aichemist-tools__write_file'.

commit.message:
refactor(validate): simplify Flux substitution to defaults + env vars + placeholders (#3480)

* refactor(validate): simplify Flux substitution to defaults + env vars

Remove ConfigMap/Secret parsing infrastructure from workload validate.
The validate command only needs schema-valid YAML — not runtime-accurate
values. Flux's ${VAR:=default} syntax already provides valid defaults.

Changes:
- Delete struct types: fluxSubstituteSourceRef, fluxKustomizationManifest,
  variableResourceManifest, validationSubstitutions, substituteSourceSet
- Delete functions: loadValidationSubstitutions, collectFluxSubstituteSources,
  collectFluxSubstituteSourcesFromFile, collectSubstitutionValuesFromFile,
  readYAMLDocuments, decodeSecretValue, applyValidationSubstitutions
- Add expandFluxSubstitutions using envvar.ExpandBytesWithLookup with
  os.LookupEnv fallback (unknown vars treated as unset so defaults apply)
- Remove substitutions parameter from entire call chain
- Remove unused imports: bufio, bytes, encoding/base64, utilyaml, yaml
- Rename test to TestValidateCmdSubstitutesFluxPostBuildVariablesWithDefaults

Reduces validate.go from 855 to 596 lines (-259 lines).

* Update pkg/cli/cmd/workload/validate_test.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* Update pkg/cli/cmd/workload/validate.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* feat(validate): type-aware placeholder substitution using JSON schemas

Replace the envvar-based Flux variable expansion with a type-aware
approach that uses JSON schemas to determine field types:

- Bare ${VAR}: looks up the schema type for the field path and
  substitutes a typed placeholder ("placeholder" for strings,
  0 for integers, true for booleans)
- ${VAR:=default} / ${VAR:-default}: parses the default value
  according to the schema type (e.g. "3" → int 3 for integer fields)
- Mixed text (e.g. whoami.${domain}): substitutes "placeholder"
  in string context

Schemas are fetched from yannh/kubernetes-json-schema for core
resources and datreeio/CRDs-catalog for CRDs, with disk caching.

This resolves the 26 validation failures when running against the
platform manifests (bare ${VAR} references previously expanded to
empty strings, producing invalid YAML).

* fix(validate): address review feedback for expansion logic

- Use YAML-aware document splitting via NewYAMLReader
- Handle list/array root YAML documents in expandDocument
- Trim whitespace in parseTypedDefault before parsing
- Remove network schema fetching (disk cache only)
- Add env var lookup support with os.LookupEnv
- Add YAML-native type inference for unknown schema types
- Add test for env var override of defaults
- Fix all golangci-lint issues

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/sessions/bccb0fc4-50c8-4b13-88a8-ca49cbab4c65

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(validate): add env override test for := operator

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/sessions/bccb0fc4-50c8-4b13-88a8-ca49cbab4c65

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[bug] Replace context.TODO() with propagated context.Context (#156)

Thread context.Context through all AWS SDK API call chains instead of
using context.TODO(). This enables proper cancellation and timeout
support throughout the codebase.

Changes:
- Add ctx context.Context as first parameter to all functions making
  AWS API calls in lib/ and config/ packages
- Replace all context.TODO() with ctx in those functions
- Update all cmd/ command handlers to create context.Background() and
  pass it through the call chain
- Update all test files to pass context.Background() to modified
  functions

The existing tgw_routetables.go already had the correct pattern and
was not modified. No per-call timeouts were added; context is simply
threaded through for future cancellation/timeout support.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: fog
repository.description: Tool for managing your CloudFormation

commit.message:
fix: abort deployment when prechecks fail with stop-on-failed-prechecks enabled (#155)

runPrechecks previously returned only a display string and used the
stop-on-failed-prechecks config solely to choose between two message
variants. deployTemplate had no way to act on the precheck outcome and
unconditionally proceeded to create and deploy the changeset.

Change runPrechecks to return (string, bool) where the bool signals
abort. deployTemplate now checks this signal and exits before changeset
creation when prechecks fail and stop-on-failed-prechecks is true.

Also enhance integration tests to verify changeset creation is actually
skipped when prechecks signal abort.

Fixes T-567

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GitHub Pages deployment via GitHub Actions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix APNs channel creation and unguarded JSON parse crashes

APNs channel creation:
- Fix request body to use correct Apple API fields (message-storage-policy, push-type)
- Read channel ID from apns-channel-id response header instead of body
- Return response headers from makeChannelRequest()
- Remove incorrect apns-push-type/apns-topic headers from channel management requests
- Fix listRemoteChannels() to use /all-channels endpoint
- Remove broken name-matching sync logic (Apple channels have no names)

JSON parse crashes:
- Add response.ok checks to fetchSchedule() and fetchRhizomePhotos() in server.ts
- Add .catch() handlers to prevent unhandled promise rejections
- Add response.ok checks to Miele getToken(), refreshToken(), getActivePrograms()
